_meta.source: IMMI-SIGNAL ICP research — composite of Amazing_Rope_4626 + Academic_Dot_8970 (r/ObsidianMD)

q1_role: Product Lead at a 50-person B2B SaaS startup (post-PMF, Series A). I own the platform product that serves operations teams. Day-to-day: roadmap planning, cross-functional alignment, customer discovery, shipping with a team of 3 engineers.
q2_goals:
1. Ship Q3 platform roadmap (3 major features) by end of September
2. Standardise how our team uses AI across the company — currently everyone is doing their own thing
3. Hire 2 senior engineers by August without compromising culture
q4_reexplain: Every single conversation I have to explain: I'm a product lead at a B2B SaaS startup, 50 people, Series A, we serve operations teams, I manage 3 engineers, we're mid-Q3 with specific deadlines. It takes 3 minutes before the AI is actually useful. Multiply that by 8 conversations a day.
q5_principles:
1. Build what you can measure — if you can't track it, don't build it
2. Async-first: decisions in writing, not in meetings
3. Strong opinions, loosely held — change your mind when the data changes
q6_projects:
- Q3 Platform Roadmap: bulk operations feature (ships July), API v2 (ships August), reporting module (ships September)
- Hiring pipeline: 2 senior engineers, currently 3 candidates in final round
- Company AI adoption: evaluating Claude Team vs departmental rollout
- Customer research: 6 discovery interviews scheduled for July
q7_people: co-founder (CEO, I report to her, she sets company strategy), 3 engineers I manage (frontend, backend, full-stack), 2 PMs I collaborate with on roadmap, designer I work with daily, VP Sales who is my main internal stakeholder, 3 enterprise clients in operations/logistics who are my primary research contacts
q8_decisions:
1. Claude Team vs phased department rollout — need to decide by July 15
2. Bulk operations architecture — sync vs async processing, affects Q3 timeline significantly
3. Whether to promote the backend engineer to tech lead (he's ready, but we need backfill first)
q9_never_assume:
Do not assume I want to move fast and break things — I value deliberate, reversible decisions
Do not assume enterprise means slow — my clients want speed and have told me so
Do not treat me as a solo founder — I operate in a team context where buy-in matters
q10_routines:
Morning: 20 min deep work block before Slack (protects thinking time)
Weekly: Friday async retro with team
Decision-making: write a 1-pager before any significant decision — forces clarity
